taxonomy: Salix vagans livida is a variant of the species Salix vagans, which is a type of willow tree. The species Salix vagans is classified within the genus Salix, which includes various species of willows. The genus Salix is part of the family Salicaceae, a group of flowering plants. The family Salicaceae is also known as the willow family or the elderberry family. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of these plants.

The medicinal uses of Salix vagans livida are not well-documented, but its close relatives in the Salix genus have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
